citations.claim: The coordinator is the central routing and trust layer in the Darkbloom network running in GCP Confidential VM with hardware-encrypted memory
Main function sets up core components including store, registry, server, billing, and rate limiting
Server struct contains registry, store, billing, rate limiters, and various configuration settings
Routes method defines all HTTP endpoints including consumer inference APIs, provider WebSocket, billing endpoints, and admin management
Consumer API implements OpenAI-compatible endpoints with streaming support and configurable timeouts
Provider WebSocket handler manages provider connections with registration and ACME client certificate verification
Registry manages connected providers with trust levels, model catalog, and request queue
E2E encryption uses NaCl Box with X25519 key exchange for request confidentiality between coordinator and providers
Billing service provides unified payment processing with Stripe and referral system
Store interface provides dual-backend storage with MemoryStore for development and PostgreSQL for production
Protocol package defines WebSocket message types for coordinator-provider communication
External dependencies include WebSocket library, PostgreSQL driver, Datadog clients, JWT parsing, and crypto primitives
Datadog integration with APM tracer and client for metrics and logs
Billing configuration includes Stripe payments and Connect Express with referral system
Step-CA root certificate configuration for ACME client cert verification
